methodology: Cross-reference network (JHN.3.14→NUM.21=9v each; 'lifted up' chain Jn3:14→8:28→12:32; 'look→salvation' pattern Nm21:8→Is45:22) + patristic reading (14/14 unanimous on Jo 3:14; 12 commentators from AD 108 to AD 1849; 4-dimension paradox framework) + CCEL (Owen justification typology, Spurgeon 3 dedicated sermons, Wesley on Nehushtan) + topic verification (BRAZEN_SERPENT, TYPES_OF_CHRIST, SERPENT)

evidence: commentaries (14 entries — ALL unanimous: Augustine, Cyril, Chrysostom 2x, Theophilus AD 184, Ignatius AD 108, Justin Martyr, Irenaeus, Haydock, Bede, Clement, Cyprian, Hippolytus, Tertullian)
commentaries (Augustine 'great sacrament', Ephrem Eden→desert→cross, Gregory 'CONTRAST not type', Justin, Haydock — 6 entries)
ccel (Wesley 'nothing but a piece of brass')

new_refs_added.justification: Already in gold_references but MISSING from relevance_judgments. 6v de NUM.21.9. Chrysostom develops 5-dimension parallel. Inseparable from 3:14 — declares the PURPOSE of the lifting: 'that whoever believes may have eternal life.'

6v de JHN.3.14. 7v↔JHN.8.28. Lapide cites 'cap. iii:14' directly. Haydock cross-refs 'John iii.14 and viii.28.' Third occurrence of 'lifted up' — completes the Johannine arc: 3:14 (as Moses lifted) → 8:28 (when you lift up) → 12:32 (if I am lifted up, I will draw ALL).

candidates_noted.justification: 6v de JHN.3.14. 7v↔JHN.12.32. Second 'lifted up' — reveals divine identity ('then you will know that I AM').
3v de NUM.21.9. Cyril cites in Jn 3:14. 'In the likeness of sinful flesh' — theological foundation of the form-without-essence paradox.
5v de NUM.21.8. Same look→salvation pattern. OT establishes the principle that Nm 21 enacts and Jn 3:14 fulfills.
4v de NUM.21.9. 'They will look on the one they have pierced.' Eschatological echo of the look→salvation pattern.

paradox_four_dimensions.description: The serpent paradox articulated in 4 dimensions by patristic commentators

dimensions.name: Inversion
dimensions.formula: Serpent (cause of death) → instrument of salvation. 'Healed of death by believing in one who died.'

dimensions.name: Form without Essence
dimensions.formula: Bronze serpent: appearance of the beast WITHOUT venom. Christ: likeness of sinful flesh WITHOUT sin (Rom 8:3).

dimensions.name: Temporal → Eternal
dimensions.formula: Bronze serpent heals temporal death. Crucified Christ heals eternal death. A fortiori: 'how much MORE.'

dimensions.name: Eyes of Body → Eyes of Spirit
dimensions.formula: Bodily eyes + bronze serpent = bodily life. Spiritual eyes + Christ on cross = spiritual life.

gregory_contrast_view.view: The serpent is NOT a positive type but a CONTRAST (negative inversion) — what died killed with it the powers subject to it. 'O death, where is your sting?'
gregory_contrast_view.significance: Only dissenting voice among 14 commentators — not disagreeing with the typology but reframing it as contrast rather than correspondence. Both readings coexist in patristic tradition.

lifted_up_chain.description: Three occurrences of 'lifted up' (ὑψωθῆναι) in John form a complete theological arc

chain.content: 'As Moses lifted up the serpent' — comparison with OT type
'When YOU lift up the Son of Man' — human agency in crucifixion
'If I am lifted up, I will draw ALL to myself' — universal scope

lifted_up_chain.confirmed_by: Lapide on Jn 12:32 cites 'cap. iii:14'; Haydock cross-refs all three

unanimous_consensus.consensus: 14/14 commentators agree on the serpent→cross typology. This is the only verse in the entire research with UNANIMOUS patristic consensus across all entries.
unanimous_consensus.earliest: Ignatius of Antioch (AD 108) — ~10 years after the apostle John's death
